demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care (nursing home) facility; bedbound, previously mobilised with a walker; high dependency on nursing staff

contact_nok.relationship: nursing home staff (present and making the call)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call placed by nursing home staff, patient present and highly distressed) 'My left leg is completely dead and freezing, and the pain in my calf is unbearable.' Staff add: it came on suddenly about two hours ago, the foot looks pale and he can't move it.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden, excruciating pain in the left lower leg with coldness, loss of feeling and inability to move the foot, not relieved by rest or the usual facility pain relief.

symptom_narrative.duration: roughly two hours and not letting up
symptom_narrative.character: excruciating, unrelenting pain in the calf and foot; the foot then went numb and now he can't feel it or move it
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: unbearable — the worst pain he can describe; he is writhing and groaning
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: left foot and calf, the coldness and numbness reaching up to about the middle of the calf
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, not eased by rest or the usual facility pain medicine
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped — rest and the normal pain relief did not touch it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it has stayed severe on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: the foot feels icy cold and looks pale; it started as pins-and-needles in the toes and quickly became complete numbness; he can no longer wiggle his toes or lift the foot
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: staff note a history of cramping leg pain on walking (claudication) and known poor circulation / peripheral arterial disease in the leg
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound and fully dependent on staff; cannot move the foot at all

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: poor circulation in the legs / peripheral arterial disease
past_medical_history.how_managed: documented in the nursing home chart

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: cramping leg pain when walking (intermittent claudication)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in residential aged care, bedbound, high dependency on nursing staff who are present and assisting

objective_data_offered.value: BP 150/85, heart rate 92, temperature 36.4, respiratory rate 20, oxygen saturation 95%, weight 71 kg
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility staff readings relayed over the phone; not independently verified

objective_data_offered.value: the left foot and lower leg look very pale/white with blotchy (mottled) discolouration, and there is a clear line about the middle of the calf where the colour and warmth change
staff say the left foot and lower leg feel icy cold to touch compared with the other leg, with the coldness reaching up to about mid-calf
staff used a hand-held Doppler and could not hear any pulse in the foot
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: hand-held Doppler used by facility nursing staff, not a vascular specialist — may be unreliable and does not replace formal assessment

objective_data_offered.value: he says he cannot feel the foot at all and cannot move or wiggle his toes or lift the foot
the leg had been resting elevated on the bed; staff have now lowered it after the call
